recommended_path: Revise the TODO/package before renewed APROVADO. The plan is directionally sound, but it needs explicit consumer-surface coverage, a canonical cleanup path, and tighter repair/force-delete invariants before the gate should proceed.

findings.title: Consumer-surface coverage is missing for changed account-profile delete semantics.
findings.rationale: The package plans a service-level guard for DELETE /admin/api/v1/account_profiles/{id} so direct deletion of the last active profile is rejected, but it contains no Frontend / Consumer Matrix or backend-only waiver.
findings.suggested_action: Add an explicit consumer matrix row for account profile deletion semantics, including whether admin UI/test harness consumers are updated, already compatible, or intentionally waived with rationale.

findings.title: Playwright cleanup repair is scoped as many site edits rather than a canonical cleanup path.
findings.rationale: D-08 expands cleanup to all current account_onboardings cleanup sites and lists many specs individually, but it does not require a shared aggregate-cleanup helper or source-level ban on profile-only cleanup for onboarding-created accounts.
findings.suggested_action: Introduce or reuse one canonical Playwright cleanup helper for onboarding-created accounts, route listed specs through it, and add source-scan evidence that no onboarding cleanup calls DELETE /account_profiles/{id} directly.

findings.title: Repair command safety contract is not explicit enough for tenant-scoped mutation.
findings.rationale: The package plans a backend-owned dry-run/execute repair command, but does not specify required tenant argument/context, idempotent execute behavior, dry-run output shape, or execute safeguards.
findings.suggested_action: Require explicit tenant scope, dry-run counts by action, execute requiring prior dry-run-compatible criteria, idempotent behavior, and durable skip/report output for ambiguous rows.

findings.title: forceDelete guard semantics need clearer wording to avoid overblocking or permanent data loss.
findings.rationale: The package says delete and forceDelete must be guarded, while also noting that an already soft-deleted profile must not make repair impossible.
findings.suggested_action: Clarify that forceDelete handling must distinguish active-profile removal, already-soft-deleted profile purge, aggregate account deletion, and repair/restoration workflows.
